beats.text: Hi, I'm Fleming John, and this is CTIN. On-chain treasuries are glass houses. The moment a fund rebalances, buys, or pays out, the whole market can see it.
Bots front-run the trade, competitors copy the strategy, and sandwich attacks alone drained over two hundred eighty nine million dollars in early twenty twenty five. So institutions stay off-chain.

CTIN is a confidential execution layer. Institutions submit encrypted intents: a buy of four hundred, a sell of three hundred fifty, a buy of one hundred. Nox nets them privately inside a trusted enclave, and only the net, a buy of one hundred fifty, ever reaches the market.
It runs on the tools you already use, unmodified. Funds never leave your Safe. A Safe module pulls only the netted trade and routes it through Uniswap. No forks, full composability.

Here it is, running live on Ethereum Sepolia. Step one: an institution connects its wallet through the standard flow, no custom wallet, no migration. Step two: the operator opens a fresh batch for institutions to join. Step three, and this is the part no one else has, before trading the institution authorizes an auditor. That address gains real, scoped Nox access to decrypt this institution's intents, and only from this point forward. Step four: the institution composes an intent, buy four hundred ETH, but the amount is encrypted on the device before it ever reaches the chain. Only an opaque handle is submitted. And only the owner can decrypt their own position, revealing four hundred, to them alone. Step five: on the public explorer, everyone else sees a single netted footprint. Never who traded. Never how much. Step six: the auditor connects. Because they were authorized, they decrypt exactly the intents shared with them, and nothing else, then export a wallet signed compliance report in one click. Confidential to the market. Verifiable to the people you choose. That is CTIN.
This is not a mock. Every step runs live on Ethereum Sepolia: encrypted intents submitted, netted, and settled, with real transactions you can verify on-chain.

Here is how CTIN compares. Confidential netting, others have that too. But execution straight from your existing Safe, real revocable auditor access, and signed compliance reports, that entire stack is CTIN alone.

Who is this for? DAO treasuries that must not leak their strategy. Crypto funds entering and exiting large positions without front-running. Stablecoin issuers managing reserves. And tokenized asset managers who need privacy with compliance.
Confidential treasury execution, on your existing Safe, with built-in auditor compliance. This is CTIN.
